examples.title: Basic Data Type Declarations
examples.description: Demonstrating wire, reg, integer, and vector declarations
examples.explanation: This example shows the most common data type declarations. Wire is used for connections, reg for procedural values, and integer for loop counters. Signed types are explicitly declared when needed for arithmetic operations.

examples.title: Number Representation Formats
examples.description: Different ways to specify numeric constants
examples.explanation: Verilog supports binary, octal, decimal, and hexadecimal number formats. The size prefix specifies bit width. Underscores improve readability. X represents unknown, Z represents high-impedance.

examples.title: Vectors and Memory Arrays
examples.explanation: Vectors are multi-bit signals with [MSB:LSB] notation. Arrays create memory structures - commonly used for RAM, ROM, and register files. Part-select extracts portions of vectors.

examples.title: Wire vs Reg Usage
examples.description: When to use wire and when to use reg
examples.explanation: The key misconception: 'reg' doesn't mean flip-flop! Use reg when assigning in always/initial blocks. The actual hardware (register or combinational) depends on the sensitivity list, not the data type.
keyPoints: wire: continuous assignment or module output
